channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Oyo State Government De-escalates Tensions After Ojoo Violence Leaves Two Dead
item.description: The Oyo State Government has appealed for calm after violence erupted in the Ojoo area of Ibadan, leaving two people dead. The unrest followed the killing of a commercial tricycle rider on Tuesday nig...

item.title: Telstra outage disrupts train services via mobile network dependencies
item.description: A major Telstra outage in Australia has disrupted train services across Victoria and New South Wales, highlighting the critical role of mobile networks in rail operations. The incident, which began ar...

item.title: The Handmaid’s Tale as a Cautionary Tale for Modern Society
item.description: Margaret Atwood’s *The Handmaid’s Tale* has long been a dystopian narrative, but its relevance to contemporary societal issues has grown exponentially. The novel’s exploration of authoritarianism, gen...

item.title: Sandiganbayan consolidates plunder and gift cases against Marcoleta
item.description: The Sandiganbayan Sixth Division has requested the anti-graft’s Third Division to consolidate a P75-million plunder case and three other charges of receiving gifts against Senator Rodante Marcoleta an...

item.title: Sam Smith Announces 2026 'To Be Free' Live Residencies in Manchester and London
item.description: Sam Smith has announced a series of live residencies in Manchester and London for 2026, marking the first time the singer-songwriter will perform in the UK as part of their fifth studio album, 'Hazel ...

item.title: Two Theatre Productions to Explore Ancient Egypt and Disability Awareness in Durban This July
item.description: This article highlights two upcoming theatre productions in Durban, South Africa, set to captivate audiences during the month of July. The first production, 'The Prince of Egypt,' is a sweeping musica...

item.title: Messi's Goal Secures Argentina's Semifinal Spot
item.description: Lionel Messi delivered a crucial goal in the final moments of the match, leading Argentina to a hard-fought semifinal berth. The veteran forward showcased his leadership and technical skills, steering...

item.title: Durham police identify cadet killed in Ontario traffic collision
item.description: Tyrone Magbitang, a 27-year-old Durham Regional Police cadet, died in a tragic traffic collision near London, Ontario, on Tuesday. The incident occurred when Magbitang was traveling to the Ontario Pol...

item.title: NATO Summit Highlights Tensions Over Ukraine, Defense Spending and Regional Conflicts
item.description: The article discusses the tense atmosphere at the NATO summit in Ankara, where U.S. President Donald Trump's combative stance has intensified debates over Russia's ongoing war in Ukraine, defense spen...

item.title: Pastor Accuses Church Member of Stealing Car Parts
item.description: Pastor Victor Kanyari has claimed that one of the suspects accused of stealing car parts from Nairobi County Chief Environment Officer Geoffrey Mosiria is a member of his church. During a church servi...

item.title: ADC Demands Independent Inquiry into Presidential Council Scandal
item.description: The African Democratic Congress (ADC) has rejected President Bola Tinubu's directive to the Independent Corrupt Practices and Other Related Offences Commission (ICPC) to investigate the Presidential F...

item.title: YouTuber Rehan Tariq granted six-day remand for blasphemy and PECA charges
item.description: A judicial magistrate has granted the National Cyber Crime Investigation Agency (NCCIA) a six-day physical remand of YouTuber Rehan Tariq in a case involving blasphemy and electronic crime laws. The N...

item.title: Cloning Technology: Progress, Challenges, and Ethical Considerations in Modern Science
item.description: Dolly the sheep, the first cloned mammal, sparked global fascination three decades ago. While initial hype predicted a sci-fi future of cloned pets, humans, and resurrected extinct species, cloning ha...

item.title: Longido Sports Centre to Develop Tanzanian Athletes under Vision 2050
item.description: The 2.5 billion shilling Longido Sports and Retreat Centre, located in Oltepes Village, is set to revolutionize sports development in Tanzania. Developed by Rabbit Star Academy, the facility is 70% co...

item.title: Super Typhoon Inday Shows Slight Weakening
item.description: Super Typhoon Inday, which has been causing significant disruptions in the Philippines, has shown a slight weakening in its intensity according to the latest reports from the Philippine Atmospheric, G...

item.title: FIFA 2026: Key Dates and Controversies in Global Football
item.description: The FIFA World Cup 2026, set to be hosted by the United States, Canada, and Mexico, has sparked significant discussions about its scheduling, venue selection, and potential controversies. The tourname...

item.title: Trump administration threatens states with federal funding cuts over election policy changes
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump's administration has implemented new rules requiring states to adopt sweeping election changes, including using a federal citizenship verification database and transitionin...

item.title: K’zee Donga Overcomes Self-Doubt to Release Debut Single 'Friday Night'
item.description: Khumbuzile Tshabalala, known as K’zee Donga, is set to release her debut single 'Friday Night' on July 10, marking a significant milestone in her musical journey. Born in Soweto, she began writing son...

item.title: Apple Issues Early Security Update Amid AI-Driven Cyber Threats
item.description: Apple has released an early security update to address vulnerabilities exacerbated by advancements in artificial intelligence (AI). The update, part of a broader shift in cybersecurity strategies, aim...

item.title: Alibaba shares rise on AI investment optimism
item.description: Alibaba Group Holding Ltd. (BABA) saw its stock price surge following reports of increased investment in artificial intelligence research and development. Investors are increasingly looking for opport...
